title: T-013 — GET /api/scheduler/runs/:id and /:id/status endpoints
prompt:
# T-013 — GET /api/scheduler/runs/:id and /:id/status endpoints

PRD: docs/prd/07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md §8.2 GET /api/scheduler/runs/:run_id, §8.2 GET /:run_id/status, §8.3 SLO
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement two GET endpoints: full detail (run + assignments + override log) <1s P95 and lightweight status poll <50ms P95.

## Implementation contract
1. Route `apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/route.ts`: GET returns scheduler_run row + scheduler_assignments[] joined with override audit; uses single Drizzle query with leftJoin.
2. Route `apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/status/route.ts`: GET returns {status, progress_pct, messages[]} only — no assignments hydration.
3. RBAC: read for Planner Advanced + Scheduling Officer + Prod Manager; 403 otherwise.
4. Org scoping: wrap handler execution in `withOrgContext(sessionToken, orgId, fn)` (FT-001 HOF) which calls `SELECT app.set_org_context($1::uuid, $2::uuid)` inside a tx; Drizzle queries do not need an explicit `WHERE org_id = ...` because RLS enforces via `app.current_org_id()` from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`. Do NOT write `WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or any other GUC pattern.

## Files
- apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/route.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/status/route.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/lib/scheduler/queries.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/__tests__/route.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a completed run, when GET /:id runs, then 200 returned with run row + assignments[] sorted by optimizer_rank ASC
2. Given a queued run, when GET /:id/status runs, then 200 returned with {status:'queued', progress_pct:0} in <50ms (assert response time in test)
3. Given a different org's run_id, when GET runs under another org session (post `app.set_org_context(sessionToken_B, org_B)`), then 404 returned (RLS via `app.current_org_id()` hides; do not leak existence)
4. Given route source is inspected, when grep'd, then it contains no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` and no `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id` — only `withOrgContext()` / `app.set_org_context()`.

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/__tests__/route.test.ts asserting 200 shape + status latency + 404 cross-org (using two app.set_org_context sessions)
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test scheduler/runs/[runId]/route'

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not return 403 instead of 404 for cross-org access (information leak)
- Do not hydrate assignments in status endpoint (perf SLO would break)
- Do not skip RLS scoping / withOrgContext() wrapping

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement GET /:id (full detail) and /:id/status (lightweight) endpoints respecting SLO + foundation app.current_org_id() RLS via withOrgContext + cross-org 404.
pipeline_inputs.details: Two routes share a query helper. Detail route does single leftJoin to fetch assignments + override audit; status route reads only the run row's status/progress_pct. Both enforce RBAC + foundation `withOrgContext()` HOF wrapping (FT-001) so `app.set_org_context()` runs inside a tx and RLS via `app.current_org_id()` enforces. Cross-org returns 404 not 403. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context). Foundation citation: Async/long-running work runs in `apps/worker` (foundation T-111); do not block the Server Action / API request path. — see `00-foundation/tasks/T-111.json`.
out_of_scope: Do not implement WebSocket push (optional per FR-07-E1-005, separate task)
Do not include WO row hydration (UI fetches via 04-PLAN endpoints)
Do not implement re-run action here (T-046)
acceptance_criteria: Given a completed run, when GET /:id runs, then 200 returned with run row + assignments[] sorted by optimizer_rank ASC
Given a queued run, when GET /:id/status runs, then 200 returned with {status:'queued', progress_pct:0} in <50ms
Given a different org's run_id, when GET runs under another org session (withOrgContext for org_B), then 404 returned (RLS via app.current_org_id() hides)

Given route source is inspected, when grep'd, then it contains no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') and no SET LOCAL app.current_org_id — only withOrgContext()/app.set_org_context()
test_strategy: RED: write apps/web/src/app/api/scheduler/runs/[runId]/__tests__/route.test.ts with vitest + perf assertion + cross-org 404 (two app.set_org_context sessions)

Do not hydrate assignments in status endpoint
Do not skip RLS scoping / withOrgContext wrapping
Foundation contract: Async/long-running work runs in `apps/worker` (foundation T-111); do not block the Server Action / API request path. (cite 00-foundation/T-111).
